rationale: Test whether the CTI universal law logit(q_norm)=A*kappa_nearest*sqrt(d_eff)+C holds for macaque IT cortex neural population responses. If A_renorm_bio = A_fit/sqrt(d_eff) ≈ sqrt(4/pi) = 1.128 (same as artificial neural networks), the law is substrate-independent.

dataset.name: MajajHong2015
dataset.description: Macaque IT cortex multi-unit responses, K=8 object categories, 256 sites, 47 trials per image

H1_correlation.description: kappa_nearest predicts logit(q_1nn) across the K=8 categories
H1_correlation.rationale: With only K=8 classes the test is low-power; r>0.50 is conservative

H2_renorm_constant.description: Renormalized slope A_renorm = A_fit / sqrt(d_eff_formula) matches universal constant
H2_renorm_constant.rationale: sqrt(4/pi)=1.128; interval is ±60% to account for finite-K, noisy neuroscience data, and possible biological deviations

H3_mae.description: Fitted law predicts q_1nn with practical accuracy
H3_mae.rationale: Same practical threshold as NLP dataset calibration

H4_substrate_independence.description: PASS if H1 AND H2 both pass: law is substrate-independent
H4_substrate_independence.interpretation: If PASS: CTI law is universal across biological and artificial neural systems

analysis_plan.population_vector: Mean firing rate across 47 trials, per image per site → (n_images, 256) responses
analysis_plan.kappa_computation: delta_min = min centroid distance; sigma_W = sqrt(tr(Sigma_W)/d); kappa = delta_min/(sigma_W*sqrt(d))
analysis_plan.d_eff_computation: tr(Sigma_W) / sigma_centroid_dir^2 (same formula as NLP experiments)
analysis_plan.q_1nn_computation: Leave-one-stimulus-out 1-NN on mean population vectors, per-class accuracy
analysis_plan.fitting: OLS of logit(q_norm) on kappa_nearest * sqrt(d_eff), separate fit per brain region (aIT, pIT)
analysis_plan.regions: aIT and pIT analyzed separately (aIT expected higher categorical coding)

expected_outcomes.IT_cortex: H1 PASS (IT is specialized for object category), A_renorm_bio in [0.9, 1.3]
expected_outcomes.pIT: H1 marginal (pIT less categorical than aIT)
expected_outcomes.comparison: If A_renorm_bio ≈ 1.128: substrate-independent law confirmed → Nobel Physics score jumps from 4/10 toward 7/10
